subject: Social Science
learning_outcomes: To know about the Indian National Congress
To identify Moderates, Extremists and Revolutionaries
To know about the Gandhian era in the freedom struggle
To know the role of the leaders of the important freedom fighters
To know about the partition of India

key_terms.definition: Early Congress leaders who believed in petitions and prayers — Dadabhai Naoroji, Gopal Krishna Gokhale
Congress leaders who demanded complete Swaraj — Bal Gangadhar Tilak, Lala Lajpat Rai, Bipin Chandra Pal
Theory by Dadabhai Naoroji that India's wealth was being drained to England
Self-rule / Independence — declared as birthright by Bal Gangadhar Tilak
Gandhi's first major movement (1920) — boycott of British institutions
Gandhi's second major movement (1930) — began with Dandi Salt March
Gandhi's 1942 movement with the call 'Do or Die'
Movement by Ali brothers to protect the Turkish Khalif; supported by Gandhi

important_dates.event: Indian National Congress founded by A.O. Hume
Civil Disobedience Movement — Dandi Salt March

mcqs.question: The Founder of Indian National Congress was
mcqs.explanation: A.O. Hume founded the Indian National Congress in 1885

mcqs.question: The Haripura session of Indian National Congress was presided over by
mcqs.explanation: Subhash Chandra Bose presided over the Haripura Congress session in 1938

mcqs.question: Gandhiji started the Champaran Movement to support the
mcqs.explanation: Gandhi launched the Champaran Movement in 1917 to support indigo farmers

mcqs.question: The Drain theory of wealth was propagated by
mcqs.explanation: Dadabhai Naoroji propagated the Drain Theory in his book 'Poverty and Un-British Rule in India'

mcqs.question: The movement led by the Ali brothers was
mcqs.explanation: Muhammad Ali and Shaukat Ali (the Ali brothers) led the Khilafat Movement

one_mark_questions.question: When was the Indian National Congress founded?
Who was the President of the Lahore Session of Congress?
In which year did the Quit India Movement take place?
Who was the founder of the Forward Block Party?
Who was the last Viceroy of India?

three_mark_questions.question: Explain the role of the Moderates in the freedom struggle of India.
answer: Presenting demands to the government through prayers and petitions
Leaders: Dadabhai Naoroji, M.G. Ranade, Gopal Krishna Gokhale, Surendranath Banerjee
Believed in British rule and justice
Demanding that relief programs be implemented
Said that India's wealth was flowing to England

three_mark_questions.question: Explain the Quit India Movement.
answer: Congress called for the Quit India Movement in 1942
Gandhiji gave the call of 'Do or Die'
Gandhiji and many leaders were arrested by the British government
Jayaprakash Narayan took over the leadership
Muslim League did not participate in the movement
